title: Student Visa - New Application
eligibility: Accepted into a UAE-licensed educational institution; age 18 or above, or a sponsor for minors. Outstanding overstay fines must be cleared first.
documents: Passport copy valid 6+ months, passport-size photo with white background, acceptance letter from institution, medical fitness certificate, Emirates ID application form.
steps: 1) Institution sponsors entry permit via ICP. 2) Complete medical fitness test after entry. 3) Submit biometrics at ICP service center for Emirates ID. 4) Residence visa stamped in passport via GDRFA.
fees: Entry permit approx 500 AED, medical test approx 350 AED, Emirates ID approx 370 AED, visa stamping approx 650 AED. Total estimate approx 1870 AED.
processing_time: Approximately 15 days total across all steps.

title: Golden Visa - Long Term Residence
eligibility: Investors, entrepreneurs, specialized talents, scientists, outstanding students, and humanitarian pioneers may qualify depending on category-specific criteria such as investment value or professional achievement.
documents: Passport copy, passport photo, proof of category eligibility (e.g., investment proof, degree certificates, employment contract for specialized talent), medical fitness certificate, Emirates ID application.
steps: 1) Determine eligibility category. 2) Submit nomination or application through ICP or relevant authority. 3) Approval and entry permit issuance. 4) Medical test and Emirates ID biometrics. 5) Visa stamping, valid for 5 or 10 years depending on category.
fees: Fees vary by category and are generally higher than standard residence visas; typically several thousand AED depending on the route. Subject to change.
processing_time: Varies by category, typically several weeks for review and approval.

title: Visit Visa / Tourist Visa
eligibility: Open to most nationalities; some nationalities are eligible for visa-on-arrival, others must apply in advance through an airline, hotel, or tour operator sponsor.
documents: Passport copy valid 6+ months, passport photo, return ticket, hotel booking or host details.
steps: 1) Apply through airline, hotel, or authorized typing center. 2) Receive e-visa via email. 3) Present visa and passport on arrival.
fees: Approximately 350-650 AED depending on duration (30 or 60 days) and single/multiple entry type. Subject to change.

title: Convert Foreign Driving License to UAE License
eligibility: Applicant must hold a valid residence visa and a valid driving license from one of the countries on the RTA's accepted conversion list. Licenses from non-listed countries require taking lessons and a test.
documents: Original foreign driving license, passport copy, residence visa copy, Emirates ID, passport-size photo, eye test certificate.
steps: 1) Complete an eye test at an approved center. 2) Submit application and documents at RTA service center. 3) Pay applicable fees. 4) Receive UAE driving license, typically same day if from an accepted country.
fees: Approximately 100-200 AED for knowledge test and license issuance, plus eye test fee around 100 AED. Subject to change.
processing_time: Same day for eligible conversions from listed countries.

title: Mainland Business Trade License
eligibility: Applicant must determine a legal business activity and structure; certain activities require additional approvals from specific authorities (e.g., health, education).
documents: Passport copies of all partners, initial approval certificate, trade name reservation certificate, Memorandum of Association if applicable, tenancy contract (Ejari) for office space.
steps: 1) Choose business activity and legal form. 2) Reserve trade name. 3) Obtain initial approval from the Department of Economy. 4) Draft MOA if needed. 5) Secure office space and Ejari registration. 6) Submit final application and pay license fees.
fees: Varies widely by activity and emirate, commonly ranging from approximately 10,000-15,000+ AED depending on business activity and office space. Subject to change.
processing_time: Typically 1-2 weeks once all documents are ready, longer if additional regulatory approvals are required.
